response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, including C++ (as seen in National Instruments role), but lacks recent and deep involvement in C++ for industrial automation or cloud/platform solutions. Their expertise leans heavily toward web, mobile, and scripting languages (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang), with limited exposure to secure development lifecycle (SDL) or industrial cybersecurity standards like IEC 62443. Their architecture and mentorship skills are strong, but the role requires deeper C++ and industrial automation domain knowledge.
strengths: Strong C++ experience (National Instruments) with low-latency and concurrent programming
Proven track record in architectural design and mentorship (Altom Consulting, GISCollective)
Diverse technical background with expertise in multiple languages and paradigms
concerns: Limited recent C++ work (last significant role was in 2015) and no direct experience with industrial automation protocols
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443 or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices
Cloud platform experience is minimal (no Azure or major cloud provider exposure)

key_factors.factor: Cloud platform (Azure) and industrial automation experience
